rationale: Both confirm the core codes.audio/tts_postprocess fix is correct and surface valid, well-grounded non-blocking issues (X: unconditional containment fallback loosening all callers, and the near-useless min() short_text gate — both precise and actionable). Y goes further with an evidence-cited sweep and lands on the legacy-audio-key-not-popped/precedence-untested issue, which closely tracks the ground truth's minor comment about confirming whether the legacy audio fallback still has a live producer — something X never touches. Neither candidate catches the docstring-still-describes-old-contract comment, though that appears already resolved in the diff shown. Y's extra findings (docstring suggestion, duplicate helper logic) are valid but lower-value, and its output is marred by irrelevant leftover skill-candidate text unrelated to this PR.
